frame_label: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
generated_comment: Father did what any responsible parent will do. Zamaana theek illa, and girls also have to understand safety is not a joke. If something had happened on the way, same people would ask why she was out like that and with whom. Family restrictions may feel strict, but it is for protection only, not against freedom.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women’s safety as their own and their family’s responsibility rather than addressing male violence. It uses protective language to justify restricting daughters and includes victim-questioning about why she was out and with whom.
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Harish. Age 31. Single. A man in Chennai. You are posting anonymously on a Tamil Facebook group after a video went viral of a young woman being stopped by her father and brothers at a bus stand and forcibly taken home because she was trying to leave with her boyfriend. Most comments are supporting the father. You are adding your comment and explaining why you think the father was right and what would have happened if he had not intervened and what daughters owe their families.
